task_completion.description: Agent logged into Rocket.Chat using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible, indicating a successful login (e.g., workspace visible, no login screen).
The channel header clearly shows '#release-updates' and the chat history for this channel is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent starred the message for release 7.8.5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A star icon is highlighted or visible next to the 7.8.5 release message in the chat history, or the message appears in the 'Starred Messages' sidebar.

task_completion.description: Agent reacted to the 8.0.2 message with the warning emoji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A warning emoji (⚠️) reaction is visible attached to the 8.0.2 release message.

task_completion.description: Agent set the #release-updates channel announcement to the required notice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The channel announcement bar or room info panel displays exactly: 'NOTICE: Release 8.0.2 rollback in progress. Do not deploy. See #rollback-8-0-2-coordination'.

task_completion.description: Agent created a public channel named 'rollback-8-0-2-coordination'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar shows '#rollback-8-0-2-coordination' in the channel list, or the agent is actively viewing the newly created channel.

task_completion.description: Agent set the description of the new rollback channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The room info panel or channel header for '#rollback-8-0-2-coordination' shows the description 'Coordinating rollback from 8.0.2 to 7' (or '7.8.5' if the agent completed the truncated text).

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Rocket.Chat web UI, rather than executing API requests via a terminal or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows the Rocket.Chat instance address, and the UI elements (menus, sidebars, modals) are authentic and responsive.

integrity.description: Agent did not mock the results in a text editor or local HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The environment matches a live browser session of Rocket.Chat, not a text editor or a static, disconnected webpage.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target version 7.8.5 message is present in the #release-updates channel. Note: The task is critically defective because the 8.0.2 message does not exist in the environment (due to an outdated snapshot), so the agent cannot react to it. Score the reaction step strictly based on visual evidence; if the agent fails to react because the message is missing, they do not receive points for that specific step. The required rollback channel name is 'rollback-8-0-2-coordination'.
